title: Threat Modeling, Privacy Engineering, and Abuse-Resistant Design · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs threat modeling, privacy engineering, and abuse-resistant design from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.
Prove deny-by-default resource and action authorization across interface, service, data, cache, job, event, export, and support paths.
Analyze data action, affected people, likelihood, problematic consequence, controls, tradeoffs, and governance across the lifecycle.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Threat Model System · changed case · Review Threat Model System
steps.instruction:
review case — Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Which action demonstrates Threat Model System without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Threat Model System · changed case · Review Threat Model System.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk. For Threat Model System · changed case · Review Threat Model System, keep Threat Model System connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Threat Model System condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NACE Career Readiness Competencies — Controls reflection and evidence for career and self-development, communication, critical thinking, leadership, professionalism, teamwork, technology, and inclusive practice.

options.text: For threat model system · changed case, apply Threat Model System without its stated preconditions, omit the threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Threat Model System for threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define reporting, triage, severity, containment, remediation, disclosure, dependency response, verification, and learning ownership.
